sections.paragraph: prop. (as in Talmudic) to patch, to sew together; figuratively, to frame lies, comp. δόλον ῥάπτειν, suere dolos, Psa 119:69 Job 13:4. Elliptically, Job 14:17, וַתִּטְפֹּל עַל־עֲֹונִי “and thou devisest (false things) upon my iniquity,” i.e. thou increasest my sins with false charges. Compare a very similar passage, Deu 1:1 [Targ.] Jon. טְפַלְתּוּן עֲלֹוי מִלֵּי שִׁקְאָ “ye devise (and would add) upon him words of falsehood.” (Arabic طفّل to frame speech artfully, compare Gr. ῥάπτειν ἔπη, whence ῥαψῳδός.)
